messages.content:
When education systems are influenced by contextual (local) factors that affect educational outcomes we refer to this process as ____.
Select one:
a. Divergence
b. Privatization
c. Postmodernism
d. Globalization
a. Divergence

People with the duty to search, record and report past events are called _____.
Select one:
a. Educationalist
b. Historians
c. Philosophers
d. Sociologist
b. Historians

elect the answer that aligns with the value of understanding history as a prospective educator.
Select one:
a. Educators who are self-aware are able to development personality and make lasting inner change.
b. The understanding of philosophy enhances your ability to evaluate and resolve problems.
c. The understanding of how past events have shaped and are still shaping the present education system.
d. National Party Understanding society enables one to develop skills and values to function effectively within society.
c. The understanding of how past events have shaped and are still shaping the present education system.

hilosophers typically divide knowledge into three categories. Select the answer that does NOT form part as a type of knowledge.
Select one:
a. Propositional knowledge
b. Practical knowledge
c. Prior knowledge
d. Knowledge by acquaintance
c. Prior knowledge

The practice of training people to obey rules or a code of behaviour, using punishment to correct disobedience refers to ____.
Select one:
a. Discipline
b. Ubuntu
c. Punishment
d. Violence
a. Discipline

a. The activation of constructive and creative forces.
b. Developing people to be able to act on their own values and interests.
c. The assimilation and the transmission of culture and traditions.
d. The development of new social patterns.
b. Developing people to be able to act on their own values and interests.

a. Equipped youths with 21st century skills.
b. It was compulsory and free for all children.
c. Education carried a strong sense of cultural heritage and preservation.
d. Every old man or woman was a reference library and a resource centre.
c. Education carried a strong sense of cultural heritage and preservation.

Select one that does NOT apply. Examples of social institutions include ____.
Select one:
a. Self
b. Religion
c. Family
d. School
a. Self

In South Africa, colonization began with the ____.
Select one:
a. Bantu
b. British Colony
c. Dutch East India
d. Foreigners
c. Dutch East India

With regards to citizenship, the ______ classified South African citizens into four different race groups: Whites, Coloureds, Indians, and Africans.
Select one:
a. Bill of Right
b. Population Registration Act (Act No. 30 of 1950)
c. Constitution of South Africa
d. Citizenship Act
b. Population Registration Act (Act No. 30 of 1950)

Hirst and Peters (1970: 128) describe the four features of punishment. Select the answer which does NOT feature as punishment.
Select one:
a. Inflicts pain or unpleasantness.
b. Teaching learners’ skills you expect them to have for good behavior.
c. It is a response of a teacher to a learner who has broken certain rules.
d. Is administered by someone in authority.
b. Teaching learners’ skills you expect them to have for good behavior.

The economic system that provides framework in which international trade and business operate refers to ___.
Select one:
a. Convergence
b. Privatization
c. Neo-liberalism
d. Divergence
c. Neo-liberalism

The ____ theory assumes the family as the primary agent of socialization.
Select one:
a. Patriotism
b. Symbolic approach
c. Structural functionalism
d. Conflict theory
c. Structural functionalism

lect the example which represents an African indigenous education practice.
Select one:
a. Farming
b. Computing
c. Coding
d. Physical Education
a. Farming

Complete the following sentence. In South Africa, curriculum aims are largely determined by the ____ government.
Select one:
a. National
b. Provincial
c. Local
d. Traditional
a. National

Select ONE answer that does NOT represent an element of culture.
Select one:
a. Religion
b. Ubuntu
c. Dress
d. Language
b. Ubuntu

Education under British colonial rule in the Cape were between the years ____.
Select one:
a. 1843-1899
b. 1807-1910
c. 1994-2023
d. 1902 -1948

The freedom from external and internal restraints as a prerequisite for self-governance refers to ____.
Select one:
a. Indoctrination
b. Socialization
c. Autonomy
d. Philosophy
c. Autonomy

Select ONE point which DOES NOT represent the three points worth noting about Locke’s characteristics of the state of nature.
Select one:
a. The state of nature is a state of perfect freedom.
b. The state of nature is also a state of equality
c. The nature of colonization.
d. The state of nature centers on a belief in God.
c. The nature of colonization.

he _____ theory focuses on how individual people behave in particular social settings.
Select one:
a. Symbolic interactionism
b. Conflict theory
c. Conflict management
d. Structural functionalism
a. Symbolic interactionism

The study of the origin, development, organisation and functioning of human social behaviours that are affected by social interactions refers to ___.
Select one:
a. Philanthropy
b. Sociology
c. Philosophy
d. History
b. Sociology

The main theories embedded in sociology of education are ____.
Select one:
a. Functionalism and the theory of symbolic interactionism
b. Conflict theory and the theory of symbolic interactionism
c. Marxism
d. Functionalism, conflict theory, and the theory of symbolic interactionism
d. Functionalism, conflict theory, and the theory of symbolic interactionism

Select the answer that classifies as a genre or phase of African philosophy of education.
Select one:
a. Négritude
b. Ubuntu
c. Collaborative learning
d. Coding
a. Négritude

Not flaggedFlag question
Question text
Examples of theorists under the conflict theory include ____.
Select one:
a. Max Weber; George Herbert Mead, and Charles Horton Cooley.
b. Karl Marx, W. E. B. DuBois, and Wright Mills
c. Auguste Comte; Emile Durkheim; Herbert Spencer; Talcott Parsons; and Robert Merton
d. Erving Goffman; George Homans; and Peter Blau
b. Karl Marx, W. E. B. DuBois, and Wright Mills

The period between ___ is when the National Party came to power in South Africa.
Select one:
a. 1902 -1948
b. 1994-2023
c. 1807-1910
d. 1843-1899

During the time of indigenous education in South Africa, formal education usually began after ____.
Select one:
a. Infancy
b. Retirement
c. Childhood
d. Puberty
d. Puberty

When a person holds certain doctrines or beliefs regardless of whether they are true or false, it means they have been ____.
Select one:
a. Colonized
b. Socialized
c. Indoctrinated
d. Motivation
c. Indoctrinated

ducation under British colonial rule in Natal were between the years ____.
Select one:
a. 1843-1899
b. 1843-1899
c. 1902 -1948
d. 1807-1910
